mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods or APIs found in batch 1. Batch 1 contains only UI metadata JSON files with locators for navigation, authentication, and dashboard elements. Printer enrollment functionality likely exists in Python helper files or API utility modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription verification methods or API helpers found in batch 1. This batch contains only UI element locators. Subscription state verification methods likely exist in API helper files in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in batch 1. This batch contains only UI locators. Status verification APIs likely exist in helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.ui_element_description: Pause Instant Ink button to temporarily suspend subscription service, has data-testid='pause-ii-plan-link-update-plan'
components_found_in_this_batch.access_pattern: Direct element selection via XPath or data-testid attribute
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This locator directly targets the Pause Instant Ink button required by precondition 4. The button is used to trigger the pause plan action which is a prerequisite for the test scenario.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Pause Instant Ink button to temporarily suspend subscription service
matched_locators.relevance_reason: This locator is needed to fulfill precondition 4 which requires clicking the Pause Plan button to trigger pause plan state before the test begins.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Print and Payment History navigation item to view historical usage and billing
matched_locators.relevance_reason: This navigation link is needed for steps 1 and 4 which require navigating to the Print and Payment History page. This is the primary navigation element to reach the target page where the billing cycle card is located.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Primary Sign In button that submits the authentication form to log the user into the HP Instant Ink application
matched_locators.relevance_reason: While not explicitly stated in preconditions, user authentication is an implied prerequisite for accessing the HP Instant Ink dashboard and Print and Payment History page. This sign-in button would be used during test setup to authenticate the user.

matched_constants.relevance_reason: Provides the base URL for the HP Instant Ink application which is needed for navigation in steps 1 and 4, and for initial setup in preconditions.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: Batch 1 provides minimal coverage for test case C44873414. Only 3 locators are relevant: (1) pause_instant_ink_button for precondition 4, (2) print_payment_history_nav for navigation in steps 1 and 4, and (3) sign_in_button for implied authentication setup. The batch contains high-level navigation and dashboard elements but lacks all Print and Payment History page-specific elements needed for the core test functionality including billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, info icons, page usage text, and all verification elements. The test requires 47 unique UI elements according to the test case analysis, but batch 1 only provides 3 relevant elements (6% coverage). The missing elements are likely in other batches containing Print and Payment History page metadata. Additionally, batch 1 contains no Python code files with methods, helpers, or API utilities - only JSON metadata files with locators. Backend operations like time manipulation, print simulation, and subscription state verification require API helpers and utility methods that are not present in this batch.
